questions.question: What is a ratio?
questions.correct_answer: A comparison of two quantities using division
questions.explanation: Ratios show the relationship between two quantities.

questions.question: What is a proportion?
questions.explanation: Two ratios are proportional when their cross products are equal.

questions.question: How can we check whether two ratios are proportional?
questions.explanation: If the cross products are equal, the ratios are proportional.

questions.question: What is a map scale?
questions.correct_answer: The relationship between distance on a map and actual distance on the ground
questions.explanation: Map scales help represent large distances in a small space.

questions.question: What does a scale of 1 : 6000000 represent?
questions.correct_answer: 1 cm on the map represents 60 km in reality
questions.explanation: It converts map distance to real-world distance.

questions.question: What is a multi-term ratio?
questions.correct_answer: A ratio that compares more than two quantities

questions.question: What does simplifying a ratio mean?
questions.correct_answer: Reducing the ratio to its simplest form
questions.explanation: Divide all terms by their greatest common divisor.

questions.question: What is meant by dividing a quantity in a given ratio?
questions.correct_answer: Distributing a total amount according to the ratio parts
questions.explanation: Each part is found using the fraction of the total.

questions.question: What is a pie chart?
questions.correct_answer: A circular diagram representing data using sectors
questions.explanation: Each sector shows a proportion of the total data.

questions.question: What is the total angle of a pie chart?
questions.explanation: The entire circle represents the total data.

notes.title: Ratio Concept
notes.content: Ratios compare quantities and are used in many real-life situations such as mixtures and sharing.

notes.title: Map Scale
notes.content: Map scales allow us to calculate real distances from measurements on maps.

notes.title: Simplifying Ratios
notes.content: Simplifying ratios helps express comparisons clearly and makes calculations easier.

notes.title: Division in Ratio
notes.content: Quantities can be divided into proportional parts using ratios.

notes.title: Pie Charts
notes.content: Pie charts visually represent parts of a whole using circular sectors.

facts.fact: Two ratios are proportional if their cross products are equal.
The total angle of a pie chart is always 360°.
Direct proportion means the ratio of two quantities remains constant.
Inverse proportion means the product of two quantities remains constant.
